title: hebe divaricata
intro: Hebe divaricata, also known as the native or white hebe, is a flowering plant species native to New Zealand and southeastern Australia. It is a small, woody shrub with white or pale pink flowers, often used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. The plant contains flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Hebe divaricata can be prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is often used to alleviate digestive issues, relieve stress, and boost the immune system.
